rulings.Case Title: SELMA OZER VS. FRANZ ZENZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft Flour Intercontinental Inc. (1) To Provide Further Verified Resposnes To Form Interrogatories And (2) Pay Pltf The Fees And Costs Necessarily Incurred In Connection With This Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 14, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF SELMA OZER'S Motion To Compel Defendant Flour Intercontinental Inc. (1) To Provide Further Verified Resposnes To Form Interrogatories And (2) Pay Plaintiff The Fees And Costs Necessarily Incurred In Connection With This Motion. As to Form Interrogatory 1.1 - Denied, there is no requirement that the person verifying discovery responses on behalf of a corp. must be listed as a person having prepared the responses. As to Form Interrogatory 3.7 - Granted, but limited to names and information of registrations and licenses of the corp. in Afghanistan in the last 5 years. As to Form Interrogatory 4.1 - Denied. The answer is suffificent. As to Form Interrogatory 9.1 - Denied. The question is not applicable to the claims in this case. As to Form Interrogatory 12.1 - Denied. There is no showing that any witnesses have been purposefully omitted. As to Form Interrogatories 12.2, 12.3, 12.4 and 12.6 - granted. Defendant s must provide a privilege log naming witnesses, form of evidence (statement report, photo, etc) date of item and the privilege claim asserted. Plaintiff's request for sanctions denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SELMA OZER VS. FRANZ ZENZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motiuon For Issue And Evidence Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Written Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 14, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANTS FLUOR CORPORATION, FLUOR INTERNATIONAL, INC, FLUOR INTERCONTINENTAL, INC'S Motion For Issue And Evidence Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, For An Order Compelling Further Responses To Written Discovery. Motion to compel further responses denied. Plaintiff has no further evidence to produce. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JASON MILLER VS. ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 14, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE, INC'S Motion For Dismissal Of Sanctions. Motion to dismiss the action denied. However, the court orders the following sanctions for plaintiff's willful destruction of critical evidence: At trial the court will give the Willful Suppression of Evidence Instruction (Cal. Civil Jury Inst. No. 204) to the jury, along with plaintiff's deposition testimony regarding his willful destruction of computer files and his reason for doing so. Said deposition testimony will also include portions related to the plaintiff's knowledge that the computers were going to be inspected by defendant's forensic experts. Any disputes regarding what portions of the deposition to be read shall be resolved by the trial court. =(302/LMG)
